title: ballota nigra subsp. meridionalis
intro: Ballota nigra subsp. meridionalis, also known as Meridional Black Horehound, is a subspecies of the Black Horehound plant, which is a flowering plant in the mint family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its antibacterial and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Meridional Black Horehound include relief from coughs, colds, and indigestion, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Ballota nigra subsp. meridionalis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
